title: astragalus echinus
intro: Astragalus echinus, also known as the milk vetch, is a perennial legume plant that belongs to the genus Astragalus, commonly found in the mountainous regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic properties, which help to balance the body's response to stress and promote overall well-being. Astragalus echinus is believed to have various health benefits, including improving immune function, reducing inflammation, and enhancing cardiovascular health. It is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and can also be consumed as a supplement or tea, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
